caption: Figure 1: Overview of the proposed three-stage pipeline for automated academic illustration generation.
content: We propose a three-stage pipeline for automated academic illustration. In Stage 1 (Retrieval), the system identifies relevant reference diagrams from a curated database. In Stage 2 (Planning), a vision-language model analyzes the references and generates a detailed textual description of the target diagram. In Stage 3 (Generation), an image generation model renders the final illustration based on the textual description. A critic module provides feedback for iterative refinement.
visual_intent: Figure 1: Overview of the proposed three-stage pipeline for automated academic illustration generation.
target_diagram_desc0:
The figure should be a clean, professional, and visually structured horizontal flowchart set against a pure white background with subtle light gray (hex: #F5F5F5) grid lines in the background to enhance readability without distraction. The overall layout is horizontally oriented, spanning the full width of the canvas with evenly spaced components aligned along a central horizontal axis.

At the top center, in bold sans-serif font (e.g., Helvetica Neue, 14pt), display the title “Automated Academic Illustration Pipeline” in dark charcoal gray (hex: #2D2D2D). This title sits above the main diagram, centered and slightly elevated for visual hierarchy.

The pipeline consists of **three primary stages**, each represented by a distinct rectangular block, connected sequentially by thick, solid arrows (line weight: 3pt, color: #2D2D2D). Each stage is labeled with a large numeral inside a circular badge (radius: 8pt, filled with soft blue (#E6F2FF), border: 1.5pt solid #4A90E2) positioned at the top-left corner of the block. The blocks themselves are rounded rectangles (corner radius: 8pt), filled with very light pastel blue (#F0F8FF), and have a thin stroke (1.5pt, color: #B3D9FF). The text within each block is centered, in a medium-weight sans-serif font (12pt), dark gray (#2D2D2D).

---

**Stage 1 – Retrieval:**  
- Positioned on the far left.  
- Contains the label: “Retrieval: Identify relevant reference diagrams from curated database.”  
- Inside this block, place a small icon (12pt diameter) at the bottom-center: a stylized database symbol (a stack of three horizontal cylinders with a magnifying glass overlaying the top one), colored in navy blue (#003366).  
- A dashed line (1pt, color: #C8C8C8) extends upward from the block to a small floating label in a light yellow rectangle (fill: #FFFFE6, border: 0.75pt, #CCB86E), containing the text “Curated Diagram Database” in 9pt font. This indicates the source of reference diagrams.

---

**Stage 2 – Planning:**  
- Centered between Stage 1 and Stage 3.  
- Label: “Planning: Vision-language model analyzes references and generates detailed textual description.”  
- Inside, place an icon (12pt) at the bottom-center: a simplified AI-brain hybrid (a cloud with circuit-like patterns and a speech bubble emerging from it), rendered in teal (#007B7B).  
- A thin curved arrow (1pt, color: #666666) loops from the right side of this block upward and to the right, connecting to a small annotation box (same style as before: light yellow fill, 9pt text): “Vision-Language Model (VLM)” — indicating the core model used here.

---

**Stage 3 – Generation:**  
- Positioned on the far right.  
- Label: “Generation: Image generation model renders final illustration from textual description.”  
- Icon: A stylized canvas with brushstrokes and pixelated dots (symbolizing digital rendering), colored in deep orange (#CC5500).  
- A similar annotation box (light yellow, 9pt) floats above this block, connected by a dashed line: “Image Generation Model (IGM)”.

---

**Critic Module Feedback Loop:**  
- Positioned slightly below the horizontal flow, forming a feedback loop.  
- Represented as a circular node (diameter: 24pt, fill: soft red (#FFE6E6), border: 1.5pt solid #CC0000) with a checkmark and an exclamation mark inside (iconography), labeled “Critic Module” in small 8pt font.  
- From the output of Stage 3, a dotted arrow (2pt, color: #990000) curves downward toward the critic module.  
- From the critic module, another dotted arrow (same style) curves upward and connects back into Stage 2, indicating iterative refinement.  
- Near the connection point, add a small label in italicized 8pt font: “Iterative Refinement” to clarify the purpose.

---

**Connections and Flow:**  
- Arrows between stages are straight, thick, and solid black (3pt), pointing rightward.  
- Arrowheads are classic triangular, filled in black.  
- All labels are placed just below the arrows or near connections for clarity.

---

**Color Palette Summary:**  
- Background: Pure white (#FFFFFF)  
- Stage blocks: Light blue fill (#F0F8FF), stroke (#B3D9FF)  
- Stage icons: Blue (Retrieval), Teal (Planning), Orange (Generation)  
- Critic module: Soft red (#FFE6E6) with red border  
- Text: Dark gray (#2D2D2D)  
- Annotations: Yellow-filled boxes (#FFFFE6), brown borders (#CCB86E)

---

**Typography:**  
- All fonts: Sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica Neue)  
- Main labels: 12pt, bold for titles within blocks  
- Secondary labels: 9–8pt, regular weight  
- Italic used only for process descriptions (e.g., “Iterative Refinement”)

---

**Visual Style Notes:**  
- No shadows or gradients; flat design for clarity and academic tone.  
- Consistent spacing between elements (≈40pt horizontal gap between blocks, ≈20pt vertical offset for annotations).  
- All icons are simple vector-style illustrations, avoiding complexity.  
- The overall diagram conveys a clear, logical progression while emphasizing modularity and feedback integration.

This design ensures high legibility, professional appearance, and faithful representation of the proposed methodology.

**Stage 3 – Generation:**  
- Positioned on the far right.  
- Label: “Generation: Image generation model renders final illustration from textual description.”  
- Icon: A stylized canvas with brushstrokes and pixelated dots (symbolizing digital rendering), colored in deep orange (#CC5500).  
- A similar annotation box (light yellow, 9pt) floats above this block, connected by a dashed line: “Image Generation Model (IGM)”.

---

**Critic Module Feedback Loop:**  
- Positioned slightly below the horizontal flow, forming a feedback loop.  
- Represented as a circular node (diameter: 24pt, fill: soft red (#FFE6E6), border: 1.5pt solid #CC0000) with a checkmark and an exclamation mark inside (iconography), labeled “Critic Module” in small 8pt font.  
- From the output of Stage 3, a dotted arrow (2pt, color: #990000) curves downward toward the critic module.

**Color Palette Summary:**  
- Background: Pure white (#FFFFFF)  
- Stage blocks: Light blue fill (#F0F8FF), stroke (#B3D9FF)  
- Stage icons: Blue (Retrieval), Teal (Planning), Orange (Generation)  
- Critic module: Soft red (#FFE6E6) with red border  
- Text: Dark gray (#2D2D2D)  
- Annotations: Yellow-filled boxes (#FFFFE6), brown borders (#CCB86E)

---

**Typography:**  
- All fonts: Sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica Neue)  
- Main labels: 12pt, bold for titles within blocks  
- Secondary labels: 9–8pt, regular weight  
- Italic used only for process descriptions (e.g., “Iterative Refinement”)

---

**Visual Style Notes:**  
- No shadows or gradients; flat design for clarity and academic tone.  
- Consistent spacing between elements (≈40pt horizontal gap between blocks, ≈20pt vertical offset for annotations).  
- All icons are simple vector-style illustrations, avoiding complexity.  
- The overall diagram conveys a clear, logical progression while emphasizing modularity and feedback integration.  
- Color usage aligns with NeurIPS 2025’s "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic: low-saturation backgrounds, moderate saturation for active components, and strategic use of warm tones (orange, red) for dynamic elements like the critic and generator.  
- Rounded rectangles reflect the "softened geometry" trend, enhancing approachability without sacrificing precision.  
- Dashed lines are used exclusively for auxiliary flows (database source, model labels), while solid arrows denote primary data flow.  
- Icons are semantically meaningful: magnifying glass for retrieval, brain/speech bubble for VLM, canvas for image generation, and checkmark/exclamation for critique — all consistent with standard visual metaphors in AI literature.  
- The feedback loop uses curved, dotted lines to distinguish it from forward flow, adhering to NeurIPS conventions for auxiliary or iterative processes.

This design ensures high legibility, professional appearance, and faithful representation of the proposed methodology, fully aligned with NeurIPS 2025’s emphasis on clean modularity, narrative flow, and thoughtful color semantics.
target_diagram_critic_suggestions0: The diagram contains multiple critical errors that severely compromise its fidelity to the Methodology Section and Figure Caption. First, the title is misspelled: 'Automated Acedimic Illustrtritog Igttupiton Pipeline' should be 'Automated Academic Illustration Pipeline'. Second, the diagram itself does not reflect any of the described pipeline stages—instead, it shows four mislabeled, nonsensical nodes ('Input Pryeccainc', 'AI Rendering Engine', 'Oppt Peedine', 'Otjput Valdition') with no semantic connection to the actual methodology. These labels appear to be corrupted text (e.g., 'Pryeccainc' likely intended as 'Preprocessing', 'Oppt Peedine' as 'Optimization Pipeline', 'Otjput Valdition' as 'Output Validation'). The structure lacks any indication of retrieval, planning, or generation phases, nor does it include the critic module feedback loop. Additionally, the visual elements (icons, annotations, colors) from the detailed description are entirely absent in the current image. The layout also fails to convey a clear horizontal flow, with arrows pointing ambiguously and without directionality. The figure caption is incorrectly included within the image, violating the rule that captions should be separate. Lastly, there is no evidence of color coding, consistent typography, or iconography as specified in the detailed description. This diagram appears to be a corrupted or placeholder version that does not represent the intended method at all.

target_diagram_critic_suggestions1: The target diagram contains multiple critical errors that violate both the methodology section and the figure caption. First, the title is misspelled: 'Autonated Acedinc Illstrprdion IIItouartion Pipline' should be 'Automated Academic Illustration Pipeline'. Second, the diagram shows three stages labeled as 'Input Poplrgains', 'All Generation', and 'OUtput Refinement', which do not match the described stages in the methodology (Retrieval, Planning, Generation). These labels are nonsensical and appear to be hallucinated or corrupted text. Third, the visual elements—icons, colors, and layout—are inconsistent with the detailed description provided. For example, the icons shown (document with gears, globe, wrench) do not reflect the intended semantics of retrieval, planning, and generation. The critic module feedback loop described in the detailed description is completely absent from the image. Additionally, the color scheme and layout deviate significantly: the blocks are not light blue as specified but instead use teal and purple, and there is no indication of a database source, VLM, IGM, or iterative refinement. The diagram lacks proper flow arrows between stages and does not include any annotations or connections as described. Overall, the diagram is not aligned with either the methodology or the detailed description, rendering it inaccurate and misleading. The correct stage labels and iconography must be restored, the feedback loop added, and the visual style corrected to match the intended design.

target_diagram_critic_suggestions2:
The target diagram contains multiple critical issues that severely compromise its fidelity to the provided Methodology Section and Figure Caption. The most significant problems are: 

1. **Title Mismatch**: The title in the diagram reads 'Autonated Adcemia Illgutrttation Pipeline' — this is a typographical disaster with misspelled words ('Autonated', 'Adcemia', 'Illgutrttation') and appears to be garbled text, likely due to OCR or rendering error. It does not match the correct title from the caption: 'Automated Academic Illustration Pipeline'.

2. **Stages Are Incorrectly Labeled**: The three blocks are labeled 'Input Progkeaioc', 'AI Geneton #ZJ285D', and 'Output Renpput ReFininnD' — these are nonsensical, likely corrupted versions of intended labels. They do not reflect any stage described in the methodology (Retrieval, Planning, Generation). This indicates either a complete failure in text rendering or an incorrect template being used.

3. **Missing Critical Elements**: The critic module feedback loop, which is explicitly mentioned in the methodology section as providing iterative refinement, is entirely absent from the diagram. This is a major omission that contradicts the core design of the proposed pipeline.

4. **No Visual Representation of Model Components**: There are no icons, annotations, or visual cues indicating the Vision-Language Model (VLM), Image Generation Model (IGM), or Curated Diagram Database — all of which were specified in the detailed description and are essential for understanding the system.

5. **Incorrect Color Scheme and Style**: The blocks are dark blue (#001F3F-like), inconsistent with the light pastel blue (#F0F8FF) described in the detailed description. Additionally, there are no numeral badges, no curved arrows, no dashed lines, no annotation boxes — all key elements defined in the original description are missing.

6. **Text QA Failure**: All textual content within the diagram is either misspelled or completely illegible, suggesting a broken rendering process or misconfiguration.

7. **Layout Inconsistency**: The layout is minimal and lacks proper spacing, alignment, and visual hierarchy. It fails to represent the structured flow required by a NeurIPS-standard academic diagram.

8. **Legend/Annotation Issues**: Although the detailed description specifies no legend should be included, the diagram has no explanatory elements at all, making it functionally unreadable.

9. **Caption Inclusion**: While not visible in the image, if the figure caption were embedded in the diagram itself, that would violate standards. However, the current image appears to have only the garbled title — which may be interpreted as a misrendered caption.

Overall, this diagram is fundamentally flawed and cannot be considered a valid representation of the methodology. It must be rebuilt from scratch using the correct text, structure, and visual elements as defined in the 'Detailed Description'.

**Specific Suggestions**:
- Correct the title to 'Automated Academic Illustration Pipeline'.
- Replace the corrupted block labels with: 'Retrieval', 'Planning', and 'Generation'.
- Add the critic module below the main flow with a dotted feedback arrow.
- Include appropriate icons and annotation boxes for VLM, IGM, and database.
- Use the correct color scheme: light blue blocks, dark gray text, soft red critic node.
- Ensure all text is legible and properly spelled.
- Restore all structural elements: numbered badges, dashed auxiliary lines, curved connectors.
- Remove any unintended artifacts or noise.

- Contains the label: “Retrieval: Identify relevant reference diagrams from curated database.”
- Inside this block, place a small icon (12pt diameter) at the bottom-center: a stylized database symbol (a stack of three horizontal cylinders with a magnifying glass overlaying the top one), colored in navy blue (#003366).
- A dashed line (1pt, color: #C8C8C8) extends upward from the block to a small floating label in a light yellow rectangle (fill: #FFFFE6, border: 0.75pt, #CCB86E), containing the text “Curated Diagram Database” in 9pt font. This indicates the source of reference diagrams.

---

**Stage 2 – Planning:**
- Centered between Stage 1 and Stage 3.
- Label: “Planning: Vision-language model analyzes references and generates detailed textual description.”
- Inside, place an icon (12pt) at the bottom-center: a simplified AI-brain hybrid (a cloud with circuit-like patterns and a speech bubble emerging from it), rendered in teal (#007B7B).
- A thin curved arrow (1pt, color: #666666) loops from the right side of this block upward and to the right, connecting to a small annotation box (same style as before: light yellow fill, 9pt text): “Vision-Language Model (VLM)” — indicating the core model used here.

---

**Stage 3 – Generation:**
- Positioned on the far right.
- Label: “Generation: Image generation model renders final illustration from textual description.”
- Icon: A stylized canvas with brushstrokes and pixelated dots (symbolizing digital rendering), colored in deep orange (#CC5500).
- A similar annotation box (light yellow, 9pt) floats above this block, connected by a dashed line: “Image Generation Model (IGM)”. 

---

**Critic Module Feedback Loop:**
- Positioned slightly below the horizontal flow, forming a feedback loop.
- Represented as a circular node (diameter: 24pt, fill: soft red (#FFE6E6), border: 1.5pt solid #CC0000) with a checkmark and an exclamation mark inside (iconography), labeled “Critic Module” in small 8pt font.
- From the output of Stage 3, a dotted arrow (2pt, color: #990000) curves downward toward the critic module.
- From the critic module, another dotted arrow (same style) curves upward and connects back into Stage 2, indicating iterative refinement.
- Near the connection point, add a small label in italicized 8pt font: “Iterative Refinement” to clarify the purpose.

---

**Connections and Flow:**
- Arrows between stages are straight, thick, and solid black (3pt), pointing rightward.
- Arrowheads are classic triangular, filled in black.
- All labels are placed just below the arrows or near connections for clarity.

---

**Color Palette Summary:**
- Background: Pure white (#FFFFFF)
- Stage blocks: Light blue fill (#F0F8FF), stroke (#B3D9FF)
- Stage icons: Blue (Retrieval), Teal (Planning), Orange (Generation)
- Critic module: Soft red (#FFE6E6) with red border
- Text: Dark gray (#2D2D2D)
- Annotations: Yellow-filled boxes (#FFFFE6), brown borders (#CCB86E)

---

**Typography:**
- All fonts: Sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica Neue)
- Main labels: 12pt, bold for titles within blocks
- Secondary labels: 9–8pt, regular weight
- Italic used only for process descriptions (e.g., “Iterative Refinement”)

---

**Visual Style Notes:**
- No shadows or gradients; flat design for clarity and academic tone.
- Consistent spacing between elements (≈40pt horizontal gap between blocks, ≈20pt vertical offset for annotations).
- All icons are simple vector-style illustrations, avoiding complexity.
- The overall diagram conveys a clear, logical progression while emphasizing modularity and feedback integration.
- Color usage aligns with NeurIPS 2025’s "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic: low-saturation backgrounds, moderate saturation for active components, and strategic use of warm tones (orange, red) for dynamic elements like the critic and generator.
- Rounded rectangles reflect the "softened geometry" trend, enhancing approachability without sacrificing precision.
- Dashed lines are used exclusively for auxiliary flows (database source, model labels), while solid arrows denote primary data flow.
- Icons are semantically meaningful: magnifying glass for retrieval, brain/speech bubble for VLM, canvas for image generation, and checkmark/exclamation for critique — all consistent with standard visual metaphors in AI literature.
- The feedback loop uses curved, dotted lines to distinguish it from forward flow, adhering to NeurIPS conventions for auxiliary or iterative processes.

This design ensures high legibility, professional appearance, and faithful representation of the proposed methodology, fully aligned with NeurIPS 2025’s emphasis on clean modularity, narrative flow, and thoughtful color semantics.
